channel.title: Blockchain Texts on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Blockchain Texts on emsenn.net

item.title: Content-Addressed Storage: IPFS, Arweave, and the Permaweb
item.description:
¶The Address Problem

The World Wide Web uses location addressing: URLs point to servers, and the server’s location determines the address. This creates dependency on continuous availability and trust in the server operator. If a server goes offline, gets hacked, or its operator decides to change the content, the address breaks.

In the NFT ecosystem, this became a critical failure mode. An NFT smart contract stores metadata as a URL — typically an IPFS gateway or centralized server. The blockchain only stores the address, not the content. A study sampling almost 500,000 NFTs found that roughly 20% were broken: 76,000 had unresolvable addresses, 4,600 had broken image links, and 19,000 had IPFS links that couldn’t resolve. “Rug pulls” exploit this weakness — creators host metadata on centralized servers, then take them down when the NFT reaches peak value.

item.title: Merkle Trees, Merkle Patricia Tries, and Verifiable Computation
item.description:
¶Merkle Trees in Blockchains

A Merkle tree is a binary tree where each leaf is a data item and each non-leaf node is the hash of its two children. The root hash, called the Merkle root, is a compact fingerprint of the entire tree.

In Bitcoin, all transactions in a block are arranged into a Merkle tree. The block header stores only the Merkle root, not the full list of transactions. This enables two critical properties:

item.title: Web3 Game Economies: From Play-to-Earn Collapse to Sustainable Design
item.description:
¶The Axie Infinity Collapse: Token Inflation and Scholarship Collapse

Axie Infinity was the highest-profile play-to-earn game, reaching 2.7 million daily active users in November 2021 with AXS (the governance token) peaking at 155.Over155. Over 4 billion in transactions flowed through the game, with daily volumes hitting 1billion.InSoutheastAsia,especiallythePhilippines,playersreportedearning1 billion. In Southeast Asia, especially the Philippines, players reported earning 200-1,000 monthly through SLP (Smooth Love Potion), the in-game currency.
